data.headline: Bathroom Remodeling in Greenwich, CT
data.subheadline: Licensed Connecticut bathroom remodeling contractor serving Greenwich and Fairfield County , complete renovations from walk-in showers to full master suites.

data.title: Bathroom Remodeling in Greenwich, CT
data.content:
Greenwich bathrooms reflect the standards of one of America's most affluent communities , marble vanities, custom tile work, and upgraded fixtures are the baseline expectation. Many homes in Belle Haven, Riverside, and Old Greenwich have master bathrooms that were once top-of-the-line but are now 20 to 40 years old with dated tile, worn fixtures, and layouts that don't meet modern standards.

BuiltWell serves Greenwich with bathroom renovations that match the quality expectations of the community. Whether you're converting a dated tub-shower combo into a frameless walk-in shower or renovating a master suite with heated floors and a freestanding soaking tub, our team delivers precision craftsmanship and clear project management from start to finish.

data.title: What Is Included in a Bathroom Remodel in Greenwich
data.content:
A bathroom remodel in Greenwich covers full demolition, waterproofing, tile, plumbing fixtures, vanity, electrical, ventilation, and all Connecticut permits, coordinated as one project from pre-construction planning through the final walkthrough. Pre-war Greenwich estates have master bathrooms that are often larger than an entire bathroom in a postwar ranch, with original plaster walls, pedestal fixtures, and drain lines that have been in place for seventy or eighty years.

We begin with full demolition of existing tile, fixtures, vanity, and flooring. Once surfaces are open, we assess subfloor structure, wall framing condition, and drain line material. In older Greenwich homes, galvanized drain lines are common and cast-iron waste lines frequently show internal corrosion that creates slow drainage even when the lines appear intact from the outside. We address these during the rough-in phase rather than tiling over a system that will need excavation later.

Waterproofing is installed behind all shower and tub areas using membrane systems appropriate for the specific wall construction. Plaster substrate requires different preparation than drywall or cement board, and in a Greenwich home where the plaster may be original three-coat work, we evaluate whether the surface can carry new tile or needs to be replaced entirely before the membrane goes on.

Finish scope covers floor and wall tile, vanity and countertop installation, all plumbing fixture connections, electrical updates for lighting and outlets, and exhaust fan installation. Greenwich master bathroom projects frequently include radiant floor heating, freestanding soaking tubs with floor-mounted supply, and larger format tile that requires a flatter subfloor tolerance than older construction naturally provides. Permit coordination and a detailed final walkthrough are part of every project.

data.title: What Makes Bathroom Remodeling in Greenwich Different
data.content:
Greenwich bathrooms tend to be larger and more complex than typical Connecticut projects. Heated floors, custom shower niches, frameless glass enclosures, and upgraded stone tile are standard expectations. Many homes have multiple bathrooms that homeowners want renovated as a coordinated project , matching finishes, consistent quality, and a phased schedule that minimizes disruption.

Waterfront properties in Old Greenwich and Riverside present moisture considerations that affect material selection and waterproofing approach. We use Kerdi membrane and cement board throughout all wet areas, with additional vapor barriers in coastal environments where ambient humidity is elevated year-round.

data.title: Greenwich Building Department and Permits
data.content: The Greenwich Building Department at 101 Field Point Road requires permits for bathroom renovations involving plumbing modifications, electrical work, or structural changes. Most full bathroom remodels require both plumbing and electrical permits. Review times in Greenwich typically run 2-4 weeks depending on scope. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection as part of the project.

data.subtitle: Bathroom remodeling costs in Greenwich depend on the scope of work, material selections, and the condition of existing plumbing and structure. Ranges are illustrative for typical Connecticut projects. Final pricing depends on scope, materials, site conditions, and permit fees. Detailed written estimate provided before any work begins.

data.title: Why Bathroom Remodeling in Greenwich Requires Local Expertise
data.content:
Greenwich homes present bathroom conditions that contractors without local experience regularly miss.

Greenwich homes built before 1970 often have cast-iron drain lines, galvanized supply pipes, and original tile installations that are concealing water damage behind the walls. We assess these conditions during demolition and address them before any new work begins.

The upgraded expectations in Greenwich mean that tile layout, grout line consistency, glass enclosure alignment, and fixture placement need to meet an exacting standard. We deliver that consistently across every Greenwich bathroom project.

data.title: Our Bathroom Remodeling Process
data.subtitle: Every bathroom remodel follows the same five-step process , structured to keep projects on schedule, on budget, and clearly communicated throughout.

steps.title: Consultation
steps.description: We visit your home, assess the existing bathroom conditions , plumbing, electrical, ventilation, structural , and discuss your goals. No charge. No obligation. We identify potential issues upfront so there are no surprises during construction.

steps.title: Planning
steps.description: You receive a clear written proposal covering every trade: tile, plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, vanity, fixtures, glass, and paint. Each line item is broken out so you understand exactly where the budget is going. We include a complete project schedule.

steps.title: Selections
steps.description: We guide you through tile, vanity, fixture, and hardware selections with options at different price points. We communicate lead times clearly so everything arrives before construction begins. If a material has a long lead time, we flag that immediately.

steps.title: Build
steps.description: Construction begins on the agreed schedule. Demolition, rough plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, tile, vanity installation, fixture connections, and paint , all sequenced properly. You receive daily updates and a clean, dust-controlled workspace.

steps.title: Walkthrough
steps.description: We walk through the finished bathroom together , every tile, every grout line, every fixture, every piece of hardware. If anything needs attention, we address it before calling the project complete.

data.title: Project Timeline
data.subtitle: Most bathroom remodels take three to eight weeks from demolition to final walkthrough.

items.title: Planning & Design
items.description: 1–2 Weeks. Consultation, measurements, material selections, and detailed proposal.

items.title: Material Lead Time
items.description: 1–4 Weeks. Vanity, tile, fixtures, and glass enclosure ordering and delivery.

items.title: Construction
items.description: 2–4 Weeks. Demo, plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, tile, vanity, fixtures, and paint.

items.title: Final Touches
items.description: 2–3 Days. Hardware, accessories, glass installation, cleanup, and walkthrough.

items.question: How long does a bathroom remodel take in Greenwich?
items.answer: Most Greenwich bathroom remodels take three to eight weeks of active construction, plus material lead time. A hall bath typically takes 3-4 weeks. A full master bath remodel takes 5-8 weeks. Your written proposal includes a complete project schedule that accounts for all lead times.

items.question: How much does bathroom remodeling cost in Greenwich?
items.answer: Bathroom remodeling in Greenwich ranges from $8,000 for a half-bath update to $100,000+ for a upscale master suite with upgraded stone, radiant heat, and custom features. A standard hall bath remodel typically falls between $15,000 and $30,000. A master bath with walk-in shower and double vanity runs $30,000 to $60,000. Your written proposal includes a detailed cost breakdown by trade.

items.question: Do I need a permit for bathroom remodeling in Greenwich?
items.answer: Yes. Most bathroom remodeling projects in Greenwich require permits from the Greenwich Building Department, particularly for plumbing and electrical work. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection as part of the project.

items.question: What types of bathrooms do you remodel in Greenwich?
items.answer: We handle all bathroom types in Greenwich: half baths, hall baths, master baths, guest baths, and basement bathrooms. Greenwich's housing stock includes estates, colonials, Tudor-style, and custom-built homes. We're familiar with the construction conditions in these homes and account for them in every proposal.

items.question: Can you convert my tub to a walk-in shower in Greenwich?
items.answer: Yes. Tub-to-shower conversions are one of our most requested bathroom renovations in Greenwich. The project typically involves removing the existing tub, modifying the drain location, installing waterproofing membrane, and building a tiled walk-in shower with glass enclosure. Most conversions take 2-3 weeks and cost between $12,000 and $25,000 depending on tile selections and shower configuration.
